title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. umbellata
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. umbellata, also known as the Downy Rose or Scotch Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family native to Scotland and Ireland. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Rosa rubiginosa subsp. umbellata has been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. umbellata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
